name: Spicey Sour Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry small red chili peppers. Remove stems, but do not cut them.
2. Place the chilies into a clear preserving or jam jar. Add 2 whole garlic cloves.
3. Pour vinegar over the chilies until they are covered by about 1/2 inch of liquid.
4. Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place (not the refrigerator) for at least one week.
5. The sauce will keep for up to 2 months, with slightly smoother, richer flavors emerging as the sauce ages.
6. To serve, pour off some vinegar into a small soy sauce bowl and add a very small dash of fish sauce. You may add chopped fresh chili, shredded carrot, or diced cucumber to add color and flavor.
